note: Reviewer subagents were not callable from the agent context (no Task/SendMessage tool surfaced). The producing agent performed a structured self-review against code-writing review dimensions. This report should be re-run by the team lead with the actual dev-code-reviewer subagent before merging.

dimensions_checked.architectural_patterns: pass — formatCPUTime and buildProcPidResult colocated with reader functions in procpidstat.go. PGresult construction mirrors NewPGresultQuery in postgres.go (sql.NullString{Valid: true} for every cell). procPidResultCols declared as package-level variable; the 17-column invariant is encoded via the const procPidResultNcols which is referenced everywhere — eliminates magic numbers.
dimensions_checked.separation_of_concerns: pass — buildProcPidResult is a pure function taking all inputs as parameters (no globals, no runtime.NumCPU() call inside). formatCPUTime is also pure. Reader functions remain untouched.
dimensions_checked.readability: pass — buildProcPidResult is organized as six self-contained blocks (SQL cols, PID parse, accumulated CPU, accumulated IO, CPU rates, IO rates, query col) each with a leading comment. Helper functions nullString and delta extracted to keep the main loop readable.
dimensions_checked.error_handling: n/a — buildProcPidResult is a pure transform with no IO; it cannot fail. strconv.Atoi error is handled via validPID flag, never propagated.
dimensions_checked.type_safety: pass — itv and ticks are float64 (matches sValue signature in stat.go); cpuCount is int (matches runtime.NumCPU() return). PID parsed via strconv.Atoi.
dimensions_checked.testing_coverage: adequate — 7 test functions cover formatCPUTime (table-driven, 7 cases), first tick, IO unavailable, itv==0, Ncols guarantee (table-driven, 6 cases), two-tick known-values, invalid PID (3 cases: non-numeric, zero, negative).
dimensions_checked.dependencies_management: pass — only stdlib imports added (database/sql). strconv, fmt, strings were already in the file.
dimensions_checked.security: see dev-security-auditor — PID validation prevents path traversal in downstream procfs reads.
dimensions_checked.performance: pass — single allocation per row (sql.NullString slice of len 17), pre-sized via make(..., procPidResultNcols). values slice pre-sized via activity.Nrows. No reflection, no string concatenation in hot loop.
dimensions_checked.cross_file_consistency: pass — column names match the 17-column header from tech-spec column-formulas table. sValue is referenced in comments but not called directly (the per-process CPU formula differs from system-level — documented inline).
dimensions_checked.naming_conventions: pass — formatCPUTime, buildProcPidResult are unexported (lowercase) per task spec. procPidResultCols and procPidResultNcols use Go-style camelCase. nullString and delta helpers are unexported.

observations: TestFormatCPUTime case '100h overflow' corrected: 36006000 jiffies / 100 ticks = 360060 seconds = 100h 1m 0s, not 100h 10m 0s as stated in the task spec table. Implementation follows the spec formula (secs/3600, (secs%3600)/60, secs%60); the spec table cell appears to have a transcription error. Test expects '100:01:00' which matches the formula.
Float formatting precision: rate columns (cols 11-15) use 'f', 2, 64 → 2 decimal places (e.g. '37.50'). Accumulated IO totals (cols 9-10) use 'f', 0, 64 → integer KiB (e.g. '10' not '10.00').
Guard on len(src) > 0 before src[0].String prevents panic on a zero-width activity row (defensive; activity always has Ncols=7 in practice but the guard costs nothing).
PGresult.Cols slice is copied via copy() rather than shared — prevents downstream mutation from corrupting the package-level procPidResultCols template.
haveCPU/havePrevCPU/haveIO/havePrevIO booleans gate rate calculation. On first tick havePrevCPU=false → rate cols rendered as '0' / '0.00'.
delta() helper guards against negative deltas (e.g. backend restart with reset counters) — returns 0 in that case.
golangci-lint reports zero warnings (initial gocritic ifElseChain warning on IO rate block was resolved by switching to a switch statement).
gosec reports zero issues for the package.
